description_long: This object consists of a group of irregularly shaped, dark gray stones arranged in four parallel rows. Each stone varies in size and contour, with rounded and angular edges, giving a natural, unrefined appearance. The stones have a matte, rough surface texture, suggesting they are unpolished and possibly sourced from a natural environment. The arrangement is linear and organized, resembling a deliberate placement for use as a barrier or obstacle in an urban setting such as a park or along a pathway. There are no visible carvings, inscriptions, or artificial modifications on the stones. The overall look is utilitarian, with the stones serving as a low-profile physical barrier.
description: A set of irregular, dark gray stones arranged in four neat rows, each with a rough, matte surface and natural, unpolished shapes.
description_view_0: Four rows of stones are visible from the side, showing their varying heights and rounded, uneven surfaces.
description_view_1: The stones are seen from the end, emphasizing the linear arrangement and the depth of each row.
description_view_2: A top-down view reveals the full layout of the stones, highlighting their irregular shapes and the organized row pattern.
description_view_3: Viewed from the opposite end, the rows of stones appear elongated, with the contours and sizes of individual stones clearly visible.
attribute_placement: arranged in rows on the ground as a low barrier
attribute_pattern: No visible pattern; surfaces are irregular and naturally mottled with minor pitting.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with minor surface pitting and dullness indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil or gravel, providing a subtle visual transition between paved and landscaped areas.
